concept: Prepare / Make Ready / Arrange for Purpose
description: To make something ready for use or for what is coming—arranging conditions, gathering materials, creating readiness. 'Thilonu' names the work that precedes action: the mise en place before cooking, the training before performance, the planning before building.
sound_symbolism: The word arranges with care. 'Thi' begins with focused attention, the conscious effort of readying. 'Lo' flows with purposeful ordering. 'Nu' grounds the preparation in solid readiness. To prepare as mindful arranging.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia nuora thilonu' (I prepare food). Can specify purpose with 'lila': 'mia lila muraki thepalu thilonu' (I prepare the garden for the harvest). Can take the reflexive 'miso': preparation of self for a coming event.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Preparation is mindfulness extended into future—attending to what conditions are needed, what must be in place for skillful action.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional life required constant preparation—preserving for winter, maintaining tools, training apprentices. Readiness enabled survival.
